national_expectations.summary: After topping their group and scoring freely, the Netherlands are framed by international and Dutch media as a genuine title contender, with foreign outlets openly asking whether this side can finally go all the way, set against a nation that has reached three World Cup finals without ever winning and now expects a deep knockout run.

national_mood.summary: The mood blends fresh optimism after a free-scoring group stage with an undercurrent of anxiety about the defence, with Dutch coverage citing foreign analysis that the back line looked shaky against Sweden and still needs to improve, tempering the euphoria around the attack.

manager_situation.summary: Ronald Koeman's standing has strengthened during the tournament, with foreign press crediting his selection calls after the team clicked, though he publicly played down his side and cast Morocco as a serious test, describing it as a big game against a good, quality opponent rather than a routine win.

historical_context.summary: The Netherlands are one of football's great nearly-men, having reached the World Cup final three times without ever lifting the trophy, a history Dutch and British media invoke as the backdrop to this campaign; previews also note they remain undefeated in their previous World Cup matches against African opposition and have met Morocco before at a World Cup group stage.

dominant_storylines.storyline: Cody Gakpo's personal tragedy frames the build-up: he and his partner announced the loss of their unborn son, and the KNVB confirmed he chose, in consultation with his girlfriend, to remain with the squad. He had been in excellent form, scoring twice against Sweden, making his decision to stay an emotionally charged storyline heading into the Morocco tie.

Brian Brobbey's emergence as the central striker is a major narrative, with foreign press crediting Koeman's selection and praising the striker's strength, technique and finishing after he tore through Sweden; he is tipped to retain his starting role.

The defensive debate runs through tactical previews: foreign analysis cited by Dutch media flags a shaky Dutch back line against Sweden and warns the defence still needs to improve, with pundits noting Morocco's pace and forward threat could test a side yet to look secure at the back this tournament.

Koeman's pre-match framing of the tie became a storyline in itself, with media noting he deflected pressure by questioning whether the Netherlands were even favourites against a Morocco side that had drawn with Brazil; several Morocco players, including Mazraoui, were born in the Netherlands, adding a personal subplot.

A homecoming/reunion subplot runs through the Dutch national press: NOS frames the Morocco tie as a duel with 'Marokkaanse vrienden' (Moroccan friends), with several Oranje players relishing facing former Eredivisie teammates and ex-clubmates (the piece names Mazraoui, Saibari, Amrabat and Salah-Eddine among the Dutch-linked Morocco players), casting the round of 32 as exactly the kind of knockout night they came to the World Cup for. This adds a warmer, personal layer to the Mazraoui/born-in-NL subplot already noted in the file.

Dutch and international press elevate Achraf Hakimi to the central threat ahead of the tie: Koeman singled out the Morocco captain as the star man and a top-class right-back his side must prepare specifically for, describing an interesting game against a team that wants to attack with a full-back who can play attacking on every position, reframing the Dutch defensive concern around containing one elite individual. Players echoed wariness, with De Jong calling Morocco a 'heel sterk team' (very strong team) after watching them play Brazil.

Brobbey's rise is given a self-belief framing in the Dutch press distinct from the foreign 'Beast' angle: NOS profiles how the world now knows how good Brobbey is while he already knew it himself, with the striker insisting he had no doubts and crediting his move to a faster, more physical league plus coaching help (including from Van Nistelrooij); global pundits cited by VI single him out (alongside Dumfries) as a reason Oranje now look 'angstaanjagend' (frightening), comparing his scoring to elite strikers.

A midfield-selection question simmers in the Dutch press: Voetbalzone tracks scrutiny of Frenkie de Jong's form and a minor knock that briefly made him a doubt, with Koeman reluctant to drop him to the bench and Justin Kluivert floated as the alternative if De Jong cannot play. The internal squad solidarity around Cody Gakpo is also stressed, Brobbey saying the situation shows how close-knit the group is.

attributed_quotes.quote: I'm not sure if we are the favourite in the match against Morocco.
attributed_quotes.context: Speaking to reporters ahead of the Round of 32, casting Morocco as a serious test after the Netherlands topped Group F.

attributed_quotes.quote: This is an incredibly difficult time for our family. We kindly ask for our privacy and space.
attributed_quotes.context: Statement after he and his partner announced the loss of their unborn son; he chose to remain with the squad.

attributed_quotes.quote: This is a match I am hugely looking forward to. These are the games you want to play at a World Cup.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: Dit is een potje waar ik enorm naar uitkijk. Deze wedstrijden wil je op een WK spelen.
attributed_quotes.context: Looking ahead to the knockout tie against Morocco, several of whose players are former Eredivisie opponents/teammates.

attributed_quotes.quote: Everyone naturally sympathises with him. We are just trying to be there for him.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: Iedereen leeft natuurlijk met hem mee. We proberen er gewoon voor hem te zijn.
attributed_quotes.context: Brobbey on the squad rallying around Cody Gakpo after the loss of Gakpo's unborn son, saying it shows how close-knit the group is.

attributed_quotes.quote: No, I had no doubts. I know what I am capable of.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: Nee, ik had geen twijfels. Ik weet wat ik kan.
attributed_quotes.context: Brobbey on his self-belief amid earlier doubts about his ability, after emerging as one of the tournament's standout strikers.

local_availability_reporting.summary: Reported to be carrying a slight knock during the group stage but described in previews as having passed a fitness check and being available for selection; status reported, not officially confirmed.
Reported to be carrying a slight knock during the group stage but described in previews as having passed a fitness check and being available for selection; status reported, not officially confirmed.

gaps: Could not source a verbatim quote from Ronald Koeman via a primary KNVB press-conference transcript; the captured quote came via media (The National) reporting his pre-match comments.
No official KNVB/FIFA fact-grade source was retrievable for national_expectations or national_mood; these rest on media (discovery tier) only.

'Conceded in every group match' and a specific 'set-piece' vulnerability could not be verbatim-confirmed at the cited URLs; the defensive storyline was softened to the attributed shaky-defence framing that the sources do support.
Brobbey's 'The Beast' nickname was not confirmed at the Voetbalzone URL; the storyline was reworded to the strength/dominance language actually printed.
The National frames the group run as a 'perfect record' and references a Tunisia win, conflicting with the DB-authoritative W2 D1 (opener drawn vs Japan); DB fact retained and discrepancy noted.

Some Dutch aggregators labelled the tie inconsistently, but the Voetbalzone timing article confirms it as a Round of 32 (first in Group F vs runner-up of Group C); opponent Morocco and group result match DB FACTS.
No detailed primary reporting found on suspension/yellow-card accumulation for any Netherlands player ahead of the Morocco match.
